channel.title: MishMash Centre for AI and Creativity - Partner News
channel.description: Partner news from MishMash Centre for AI and Creativity

item.title: Human–AI Jamming: How Swarm Intelligence Can Shape Music
item.description: Researchers at the University of Oslo explore how swarm intelligence—inspired by flocking birds and coordinating ants—can enable musical collaboration between humans and AI agents.
item.author: University of Oslo

item.title: Technology is making the music industry more individualistic and less gender-equal
item.description: A University of Oslo research project reveals how digital audio workstations are transforming music production while perpetuating gender inequality in the industry.
item.author: University of Oslo

item.title: Bringing Viking ships back to life – one algorithm at a time
item.description: A PhD candidate at Østfold University College is building interactive 3D reconstruction tools for ancient Viking ships, making interpretive uncertainty visible alongside the digital model itself.
item.author: Østfold University College (HiØ)

item.title: Where Humans Outshine AI: "There is something quietly hopeful in these findings"
item.description: Researchers at the University of Oslo discovered that humans excel at navigating unfamiliar situations by actively exploring with their bodies, revealing that embodied cognition remains a foundational human capacity.
item.author: University of Oslo

item.title: NLA University College participates in national research center
item.description: NLA joins MishMash, contributing expertise in language, communication, and aesthetic subjects to explore how generative AI impacts creativity, education, and cultural heritage.
item.author: NLA University College

item.title: KMD takes on a key role in national centre for AI and creativity
item.description: Researchers at the Faculty of Fine Art, Music and Design (KMD) at the University of Bergen have been given a central role in the new national research center MishMash – Center for AI and Creativity.
item.author: University of Bergen (UiB)

item.title: We are going to strengthen AI expertise in creative education
item.description: The University of Inland Norway joins MishMash, one of six new AI research centres, to investigate how AI can promote creativity and ethical awareness in creative education.
item.author: University of Inland Norway (INN)
